text: This video is part 1 where I show you how I get started building this wheel.
This is a 20 inch wheel, 32 spokes cross 3. On this rim the spoke holes are
drilled at angles to accommodate spokes coming from one side of the hub flange
or the other.
It's going to be important to insert the spoke into the correct angled spoke hole.
So let's get started with the right side trailing spokes.
This is the hub that I'm going to use.
On this rim I'm going to put this label on the right side.
The valve hole
should line up with any markings on the hub.
The wheel will work without doing this, but as traditionally
it looks more professional. The spokes have been cut and threaded based on the
dimensions of the hub and the rim.
This wheel will be crossed three, meaning that any spoke regardless of direction
will cross three other spokes in the opposite direction.
So I start with the trailing spokes and all of the trailing spokes will come out
of the inside of the hub flange.
I'm going to put all of the trailing spokes in.
I'm going to put the spokes in the right hand side by putting the spokes in every other hole.
Position the hub such that any markings on the hub will line up with the valve hole.
Then position what's referred to as the key spoke to the first hole that's drilled at the correct angle
such that the key spoke doesn't cross in front of the valve hole.
Next I apply a little anti-seize to the threads.
Next I screw in the nipple just to the point where I can't see any threads showing on the spoke.
For the next trailing spoke it will go into the fourth hole.
I'm going to put the key spoke in the first hole.
I'm going to put the key spoke in the first hole.
I'm going to put the key spoke in the first hole.
There will be three empty holes between each trailing spoke.
Then that spoke is attached by screwing on the nipple up to the point where I can't see any threads on the spoke.
Then repeat this process all the way around for each trailing spoke on the right side of the hub.
Then repeat this process all the way around for each trailing spoke on the right side of the hub.
Then repeat this process all the way around for each trailing spoke on the right side of the hub.
So there you have it, all the trailing spokes on the right side.
Next we flip it over and we'll do all the left side trailing spokes in part two.
Thanks for watching, check out ElectricBikeBuilding.com where I share information about all my projects.
Let's build electric bikes!
